title: Tyla bags another Grammy for Best African Music Performance
bodyText: [{"type":"intro_bold","text":"This marks Tyla’s second Grammy win in the same category."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - South African singer Tyla has won another Grammy Award at the 68th edition of the global music awards.","html":"ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - South African singer Tyla has won another Grammy Award at the 68th edition of the global music awards."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The ceremony was held on Sunday, 1 February, at the Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles, United States."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Water hitmaker, whose full name is Tyla Laura Seethal, won the award for Best African Music Performance for her single Push 2 Start.","html":"The Water hitmaker, whose full name is Tyla Laura Seethal, won the award for Best African Music Performance for her single Push 2 Start."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"She competed in the category alongside Davido and Omah Lay’s Lay With You, Eddy Kenzo and Mehran Matin’s Hope & Love, and Ayra Starr and Wizkid’s Gimme Dat.","html":"She competed in the category alongside Davido and Omah Lay’s Lay With You, Eddy Kenzo and Mehran Matin’s Hope & Love, and Ayra Starr and Wizkid’s Gimme Dat."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This marks Tyla’s second Grammy win in the same category. She first received the award in 2024 for Water, which was her debut Grammy victory.","html":"This marks Tyla’s second Grammy win in the same category. She first received the award in 2024 for Water, which was her debut Grammy victory."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"TYLA holding her second Grammy award on the red carpet. pic.twitter.com/4BS9Jk3fBB— ? (@tylajpg) February 2, 2026"},{"type":"heading","text":"International recognition and national pride","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Following her first Grammy win, Tyla collected several international accolades. These included two Billboard Music Awards for Top Afrobeats Artist and Top Afrobeats Song for Water. The 24-year-old also won iHeartRadio World Artist of the Year and received her second consecutive MTV Video Music Award in September last year.","html":"Following her first Grammy win, Tyla collected several international accolades. These included two Billboard Music Awards for Top Afrobeats Artist and Top Afrobeats Song for Water. The 24-year-old also won iHeartRadio World Artist of the Year and received her second consecutive MTV Video Music Award in September last year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Deputy Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Peace Mabe, previously praised Tyla’s achievements, describing her as a symbol of South African creativity.","html":"Deputy Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Peace Mabe, previously praised Tyla’s achievements, describing her as a symbol of South African creativity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“From township beginnings to international arenas, Tyla has carried our music and identity to the highest stages. She embodies resilience, creativity and pride,” Mabe said.","html":"“From township beginnings to international arenas, Tyla has carried our music and identity to the highest stages. She embodies resilience, creativity and pride,” Mabe said."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“Her journey proves that South African artists are not only competitive, but also capable of setting global trends,” she added."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mabe said Tyla’s success extends beyond individual recognition.","html":"Mabe said Tyla’s success extends beyond individual recognition."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Tyla’s triumph is not just hers. It belongs to the millions of young South Africans who see in her story a reflection of their own potential,” she said.","html":"“Tyla’s triumph is not just hers. It belongs to the millions of young South Africans who see in her story a reflection of their own potential,” she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“It belongs to every artist who works tirelessly, often without recognition, to carry our culture forward. And it belongs to a country that continues to prove that its creative spirit is indomitable.”","html":"“It belongs to every artist who works tirelessly, often without recognition, to carry our culture forward. And it belongs to a country that continues to prove that its creative spirit is indomitable.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"}]

The 24-year-old also won iHeartRadio World Artist of the Year and received her second consecutive MTV Video Music Award in September last year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Deputy Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Peace Mabe, previously praised Tyla’s achievements, describing her as a symbol of South African creativity.","html":"Deputy Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture, Peace Mabe, previously praised Tyla’s achievements, describing her as a symbol of South African creativity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“From township beginnings to international arenas, Tyla has carried our music and identity to the highest stages. She embodies resilience, creativity and pride,” Mabe said."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"“Her journey proves that South African artists are not only competitive, but also capable of setting global trends,” she added."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mabe said Tyla’s success extends beyond individual recognition.","html":"Mabe said Tyla’s success extends beyond individual recognition."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Tyla’s triumph is not just hers. It belongs to the millions of young South Africans who see in her story a reflection of their own potential,” she said.","html":"“Tyla’s triumph is not just hers. It belongs to the millions of young South Africans who see in her story a reflection of their own potential,” she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“It belongs to every artist who works tirelessly, often without recognition, to carry our culture forward. And it belongs to a country that continues to prove that its creative spirit is indomitable.”","html":"“It belongs to every artist who works tirelessly, often without recognition, to carry our culture forward. And it belongs to a country that continues to prove that its creative spirit is indomitable.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen","html":"Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen"}]

teaser: This marks Tyla’s second Grammy win in the same category.
